msgs.text:
Turn 42 — finite-automorphism audit and standalone proof skeleton I re-read the attached problem_statement.md, mega_prompt.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md, using only the files attached to this message. The problem is the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotientsΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩,ΓH=F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩,with F=P⋊G, P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and the desired conditionπH∘Φ=πS.This is exactly the problem stated in the attached files.

The new Turn 42 point is an audit refinement:Do not prove that the inverse-limit map is an automorphism by appealing to Hopficity of P.As a plain pro-2 group, P is not obviously finitely generated, because its first graded layer carries Z2 [[G]]-coefficients. So the safer proof is: every finite filtered Nielsen datum induces an actual automorphism at the finite coefficient-and-degree level; the compatible inverse limit of finite automorphisms is an automorphism.This removes the last possible hidden global assumption.

1. Fixed orientation The proof must use one global relator orientation. The history fixedρS:=rS−1.

This is harmless because rS and rS−1 have the same closed normal closure. The finite and profinite substitutions must requireΦ(ρS)=rH.

Turn 40 already fixed this sign globally, and Turn 41 kept it in the filtered Nielsen data.

2. Finite filtered Nielsen dataLetΛ=Z2 [[G]],M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩,I=(X0,X1).

For a degree cutoff N and an open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, defineMN,J:=M/(IN+JM),andPN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite, and the subgroups PN,J form a separated cofinal system.

Turn 41 correctly replaced arbitrary finite quotient automorphisms by finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data:SN,J⊂(P/PN,J)4,with elements written(y0,y1,cσ,cτ).

The conditions are:y0,y1 have the fixed first Magnus layer given by the Turn 1 matrix

U∈GL2(Λ).cσ,cτ satisfy the finite cocycle relation encodingτσ=τ2.

The induced twisted substitution sendsρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J.The tuple is filtered: its higher terms live in the truncated Magnus algebra MN,J with prescribed linear part and higher terms in I2/(IN+JM).

Turn 41’s point was precisely that these SN,J are finite, functorial under reduction, and avoid the noncanonical reduction problem for arbitrary quotient automorphisms.

3. Why each finite datum induces a finite automorphism This is the extra audit step.

Given(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈SN,J,define the finite-level twisted substitution byxi↦yi,σ↦cσ σ,τ↦cττ.The finite cocycle relation ensures that the defining relation τσ=τ2 is respected. Thus the substitution defines an endomorphism over G of the finite-kernel extensionFN,J:=F/PN,J.

Now restrict to the finite 2-group P/PN,J. The first Magnus layer is acted on by UmodJ.

Since U∈GL2 (Λ), its reduction is invertible. Therefore the induced map on the Frattini quotient of P/PN,J is surjective. Because P/PN,J is a finite 2-group, the finite Frattini criterion implies that the restrictionP/PN,J→P/PN,J is an automorphism.Once the kernel map is an automorphism and the total map is over G, the whole mapFN,J→FN,J is an automorphism: for a point qg, the preimage has the same g-component and P-component determined by the inverse of the finite kernel automorphism.

So SN,J is not merely a set of relator-solving substitutions; it is a finite set of finite filtered over-G automorphisms encoded by Nielsen/cocycle data.This matters because it avoids any need to claim that a surjective endomorphism of the full infinite-rank pro-2 group P is injective.

4. Nonemptiness of SN,J The prior sweep supplies nonemptiness.

First, Turn 1 computed the abelianized relation rowswS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E),and built an idempotent-by-idempotent matrix

U∈GL2(Λ)carrying wS to wH. On the dangerous Ef-piece, the displayed U0 has determinant s, a unit.

Second, after the H1 -change, all non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled. The only surviving obstruction tower was the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 Magnus problem. Turn 18 closed that tower by provingCm=Lm (m≥5),so every odd primitive layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression

W↦W[2].Thus the residual obstruction vanishes to all orders.

Third, the finite coefficient tower over the local Ef-ring

R=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,has also been closed. The raw local row(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to the normalized row (2,t).The first t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4),and higher (2,t)-adic obstructions are handled by the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade. Turn 39 repaired the naive Frobenius bookkeeping by using the actual pro-2 Witt cascade, and Turn 41 used this to assert nonemptiness of every SN,J.

ThereforeSN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation (N,J).

5. Compactness, now without a Hopficity assumption

If (M,K) refines (N,J), meaning

M≥N,K⊆J,then truncation gives a canonical reduction mapSM,K→SN,J.These are honest reductions of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, so no intermediate-subgroup invariance issue remains.For any finite set of indices, choose a common refinement and an element of the corresponding nonempty SM,K; reducing gives compatible points on the finite subdiagram. Since all SN,J are finite and nonempty, compactness givesN,JlimSN,J=∅.Pick a compatible family. It yields elementsy0,y1,cσ,cτ∈P and hence a continuous cocycle c:G→P.The finite cocycle relations hold at every truncation, so they hold in the inverse limit.The compatible finite automorphisms induce a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F=P⋊G given byxi↦yi,σ↦cσ σ,τ↦cττ.It is an automorphism because it is the inverse limit of compatible finite automorphisms. This is stronger and safer than appealing to Hopficity of P.

At every finite level,ΦN,J(ρS)=rH.

ThereforeΦ(ρS)rH−1∈N,J⋂PN,J=1.SoΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup,Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Thus Φ descends to an isomorphism

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F∼

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,that is,ΓS∼ΓH.Because every finite-stage map is over G, the inverse-limit map is over G, and the descended isomorphism satisfiesπH∘Φ=πS.

HenceΓS≅GΓH.The Turn 42 repair is small but important: the proof now uses finite automorphisms at every coefficient-and-degree truncation and then takes their compatible inverse limit. It does not rely on a potentially false Hopfian shortcut for an infinitely generated pro-2 group.

ESTABLISHED: The finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data SN,J not only solve the relator equation modulo PN,J; each datum induces an actual finite over-G automorphism because its first Magnus layer is UmodJ, hence invertible, and the finite Frattini criterion applies to P/PN,J. The compatible inverse limit is therefore an automorphism as an inverse limit of finite automorphisms. With ρS=rS−1, the limit satisfies Φ(ρS)=rH, so ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains, conditional on the finite rank/base computations and the normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction recorded in Turns 1–41. The remaining task is reproducibility: write the proof as a polished note and attach checkable certificates for the finite base computations.

NEXT: Produce the final standalone proof with six lemmas: H1 Nielsen equivalence; residual restricted-Lie induction; first 2-coefficient secondary surjectivity Bd↠Hd(2); normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction; finite filtered Nielsen-data compactness; and descent from Φ(rS−1)=rH to ΓS≅GΓH.[TURN42-FINITE-AUTOMORPHISM-AUDIT-ISO]
